title: Zambia: Corporate accountability for pollution of vital water resources and the environment - Southern Africa Litigation Centre
impact_evidence: Environmental | Water Resources | Acid mine drainage -> "The collapse spilt highly acidic water into the Mwambashi River, with pH levels dropping to 1.91." || Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "On 18 February 2025, Tailing Dams owned by Sino Metals Leach Zambia Limited (Sino Metals) and located on NFC Africa Mining Limited's (NFC Mining) surface area collapsed, discharging an estimated 50 million to 900,000 million litres of highly acidic and toxic waste, including heavy metals, into the local environment and vital waterways." || Environmental | Water Resources | Freshwater ecotoxicity (heavy metals, pH changes, sensitive species) -> "Government ministries confirmed the heavy metals found to have been discharged into the Mwambashi Stream, which included iron, manganese, copper, and cobalt, as well as elevated levels of total dissolved solids and extremely low pH values." | "Later on 2 April 2025, the government further confirmed that the heavy metal contamination is far beyond the safe limits set by the World Health Organisation (WHO) and the Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO)." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "On 18 February 2025, Sino Metals' Tailing Dams (TD 15F, TD15E, RD15C, and TD15B) located within the NFC Mining's Surface Area and plant boundary collapsed, releasing a massive amount of highly toxic waste, including elevated heavy metals, into their environment..." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Topsoil degradation and loss of soil fertility -> "The pollution has impacted the community in various ways. It has devastated crops, made soils unusable, harmed income and food security and resulted in livestock deaths." || Environmental | Air & Climate | Dust generation -> "Ongoing earth-moving activities are worsening health risks by generating contaminated dust." || Environmental | Air & Climate | Air quality degradation (dust, diesel exhaust, blasting emissions) -> "Ongoing earth-moving activities are worsening health risks by generating contaminated dust." | "Due to exposure to toxic waste, the Petitioners and their families have suffered health issues such as diarrhoea, respiratory problems, and skin irritations." || Environmental | Biodiversity & Ecosystems | Impacts on aquatic ecosystems and life below water -> "The Waterways, particularly the Kafue River, support communities beyond Kalusale with approximately 300,000 households reliant on a fisheries-based livelihood." | "15 March 2025 – A river 'died' overnight in Zambia after an acidic waste spill at a Chinese-owned mine (AP Newsroom)." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> "Due to exposure to toxic waste, the Petitioners and their families have suffered health issues such as diarrhoea, respiratory problems, and skin irritations." | "Ongoing earth-moving activities are worsening health risks by generating contaminated dust." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "The pollution has impacted the community in various ways. It has devastated crops, made soils unusable, harmed income and food security and resulted in livestock deaths." | "The community faces severe poverty, with some trying to leave their residences in search of alternative livelihoods." || Social | Living Conditions & Social Fabric | Livability (cost of living, food security, communication services) -> "The Petitioners seek declarations of constitutional rights violations, comprehensive environmental remediation, equitable compensation for extensive damages, and immediate provision of necessities such as clean water, food and access to healthcare." | "The pollution has impacted the community in various ways. It has devastated crops, made soils unusable, harmed income and food security and resulted in livestock deaths." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "19 to 21 February 2025: The Zambia Environmental Management Agency (ZEMA) confirmed that approximately 75% of Leach Residue was discharged and issued an Environmental Restoration Order No. ZEMA/NRO/ERO/2002/2025 to Sino Metals for clean-up and monitoring." || Governance | Risk Management & Long-Term Stewardship | Tailings and waste governance -> "On 18 February 2025, Sino Metals' Tailing Dams (TD 15F, TD15E, RD15C, and TD15B) located within the NFC Mining's Surface Area and plant boundary collapsed, releasing a massive amount of highly toxic waste..." | "19 to 21 February 2025: ... ZEMA ... issued an Environmental Restoration Order ... to Sino Metals for clean-up and monitoring." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Accountability for accidents and environmental damage -> "31 July 2025: Sino Metals launched a limited compensation exercise through a 'Standard Deed of Settlement and Release' containing an unlawful liability clause. Many affected Petitioners received no assistance or compensation." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "An order to stop threatening and preventing the Petitioners from communicating with their lawyers and other Stakeholders, including NGO representatives." | "An order to allow access by independent experts, lawyers, stakeholders, NGO representatives, and others willing to help the affected communities"
